title: ginoria glabra
intro: Ginoria glabra, also known as the water caltrop or horned pondweed, is a type of aquatic plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Ginoria glabra can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
